meta.test_name: Brutal Critic Triad Roadmap Pressure Test

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.focus: Auth, injection, PII, race conditions, rollback

now_items_analysis.title: Kubernetes staged environments: DSV → TST → STG → PRD
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: PRD namespace exists and works. Adding 3 more namespaces adds operational surface before the single-app deployment even has a CI pipeline that would USE staged promotion. No test suite exists to gate promotion. This is building a highway before owning a car.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: 4-tier topology is premature. You have ONE deployed service (pi-local-app) on PRD. DSV/TST/STG without automated test gates is just namespace sprawl. The dependency 'Supabase environments table seeded' is circular — you seeded it to justify this item. Real promotion gates need: test coverage > 60%, helm diff on PRD, rollback manifests. None exist.

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: Staging environments ARE security-relevant — you're deploying directly to PRD without any staging gate today. But the fix is a single STG namespace with Traefik route, not a full 4-tier topology. Focus on STG → PRD promotion with rollback, skip DSV/TST until you have developers plural.
risks: Direct-to-PRD deploys are the actual vulnerability

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Solo developer. Zero test coverage to drive promotions. ROI of maintaining 4 namespaces vs 1 is deeply negative. Spend this time writing tests or shipping the portfolio homepage. 4-tier K8s is a team sport you're playing alone.
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.effort_vs_impact: High effort / Near-zero impact for current scale

critical_questions.really_critical_now: NO. PRD works. The actual risk (no staging) is solved by 1 STG namespace, not 4 tiers.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: PARTIAL. k3s validated=YES, Traefik Helm=YES, Supabase table=YES (but self-referential). Missing: CI pipeline, test suite, rollback strategy.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: LOW. PRD continues working. No user impact.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: local-k8s-ci-cd-parity (item #8) — both try to solve deployment topology simultaneously from different angles. Pick ONE.

now_items_analysis.title: Traefik ingress hardening and TLS automation
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: Traefik v3 IS deployed and partially configured. TLS certificates exist for some routes. This is real infra that needs finishing, not speculation.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.verdict: KEEP as critical — but SSH is dead
RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: Good item, real dependency for 8+ downstream items. BUT: SSH VPS timeout on port 22 means you CANNOT execute this today. The actual blocker is VPS access, not architecture. Dependency declaration is incomplete — it says 'VPS SSH access confirmed' but SSH is DOWN.
anti_patterns_detected: Dependency listed as met when it's actively broken

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: Without TLS automation, Let's Encrypt certs will expire. Without middleware chains, the ingress is wide open. Cloudflare token possibly exposed (per your state report). This is the only item where 'doing nothing' actively degrades security posture day over day. Rate limiting, CORS, auth middleware — all missing.
risks: Cloudflare token exposure = DNS hijack risk

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Can't harden what you can't reach. Fix SSH access → then this is a 2-4 hour task for basic TLS + rate limiting. Don't over-engineer middleware chains for services that don't exist yet.

critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: NO. 'VPS SSH access confirmed' is FALSE as of today. Only Hostinger web terminal available.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: HIGH. TLS certs may expire. No rate limiting. CF token exposure.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: cloudflare-dns-mail (item #6) — both touch CF/DNS layer. Should be done together in same session.

now_items_analysis.title: PortfolioManager wired to Supabase live data
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: Supabase schema exists with 14 tables. Seed data was JUST inserted (7 projects, 3 experiences, 25 skills). But the frontend still reads from hardcoded arrays. The bridge (live queries) doesn't exist yet.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.verdict: KEEP — this is the correct next step
RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: Clean boundary: Supabase is the data layer, frontend consumes it. But the implementation approach matters. Don't build a custom ORM wrapper — use @supabase/supabase-js directly with typed queries. The risk is over-abstracting the data access layer before you have 2 consumers.
anti_patterns_detected: Potential over-abstraction if a 'PortfolioManager' class wraps simple CRUD

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY in .env means the anon key ships to the browser. RLS policies MUST be in place before going live. Are they? If not, any user can read/write all portfolio data. Check: are RLS policies enabled on portfolio_projects, portfolio_experiences, portfolio_skills?
risks: Anon key in browser without RLS = full data exposure
No audit of current RLS policies mentioned

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: This unblocks portfolio-homepage-public, portfolio-data-seeding, and the entire portfolio visibility chain. 4-8 hours of work. Seed data already exists. This is the single item that most directly converts to 'portfolio exists publicly' which feeds income tracks.
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.effort_vs_impact: Low-medium effort / Highest impact (unblocks 4+ downstream items)

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES. Correct priority. Unblocks the revenue-generating chain.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: YES. Schema migrated, supabase-js available, env vars configurable.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: MEDIUM. Portfolio stays on mock data. No public credibility.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: dual-frontend-consolidation (item #4) — if you wire Supabase into the Vite app now, and later decide the portfolio should live in Next.js, you rework. Resolve #4 FIRST or accept the risk.

now_items_analysis.title: Dual frontend consolidation: Vite public + Next.js authenticated shell
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: No ADR document exists. No routing audit completed. This is a governance/architecture decision that's been deferred. The 'deliverable' is a document, not running code.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.verdict: ELEVATE — this is the architectural bottleneck
RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: This is the single most important architectural decision on the board. Every frontend item (portfolio-homepage, supabase-live-portfolio-manager, portfolio-seo-content) is implicitly betting on the Vite path. If this decision flips, all that work is waste. The anti-pattern is building on an unresolved architectural fork. Write the ADR TODAY — even a 1-page decision doc unblocks everything downstream.
anti_patterns_detected: Implicit decisions via code velocity instead of explicit ADR

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: The split between anon (Vite) and authenticated (Next.js) determines where auth tokens live, where session management happens, where PII flows. Without this decision, you risk leaking auth context into the public surface or duplicating auth logic in two places.

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Don't overthink this. The answer is obvious from the codebase: Vite=public, Next.js=authenticated. Write it down. 30 minutes. Move on. The risk is analysis paralysis, not wrong architecture.
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.effort_vs_impact: Very low effort / High unblocking impact

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES. But the deliverable is a decision document, not code. Should take 30-60 minutes.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: NO. 'Current routing audit completed' = FALSE. 'next-auth session strategy frozen' = FALSE.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: HIGH. Every frontend item builds on an implicit assumption. Making it explicit costs 30 min.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: supabase-live-portfolio-manager (item #3) and portfolio-homepage-public (item #11) — both proceed blind without this decision.

now_items_analysis.title: n8n operational documentation and pending key inventory
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: n8n IS running on k3s (41+ hours uptime reported). But no documentation, no secret inventory, no workflow export. It's a real running system with zero operational knowledge captured.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.verdict: KEEP but it's a 2-hour doc task, not a sprint
RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: This isn't engineering work — it's documentation. The architecture exists (n8n on k3s). What's missing is: which workflows exist, which secrets are configured, which are missing. An `n8n export` + secrets audit is 2 hours max.
anti_patterns_detected: Elevating documentation to 'critical' priority alongside actual engineering items

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.verdict: CRITICAL — secrets inventory is security work
SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: Unknown secrets = unknown attack surface. If n8n has stale API keys, exposed webhook URLs, or unconfigured auth, it's an active liability. The 'runbook' is secondary — the secrets inventory is the security deliverable.
risks: Unknown API keys in n8n config
Webhook URLs possibly exposed without auth

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.verdict: DO IT NOW — it's fast and unblocks automation chain
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: 2 hours of documentation unblocks: n8n-flows-hardening, n8n-whatsapp-flows, n8n-ingress-deploy. Three downstream items wait on this. Fastest unblock on the board.
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.effort_vs_impact: Very low effort / High unblocking impact

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES for secrets inventory. The 'runbook' part could be 'next'.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: PARTIAL. n8n is running but no ingress = can only access via kubectl port-forward or web terminal.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: MEDIUM. Automation chain stays blocked. n8n runs unsupervised with unknown config.

now_items_analysis.title: DNS, Cloudflare and mail hardening runbook
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: Cloudflare token POSSIBLY EXPOSED per state report. This is an active security incident, not a planned improvement.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.verdict: IMMEDIATE — but it's incident response, not roadmap
RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: A possibly exposed CF token is not a roadmap item — it's an incident. Rotate the token NOW. Then document DNS state. The fact this is sitting as a 'roadmap item' at priority_index 7 instead of being treated as a P0 incident is concerning.
anti_patterns_detected: Security incident disguised as roadmap item

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: If the CF token is exposed, an attacker can: redirect DNS, issue certificates for your domain, intercept traffic, send email as jpglabs.com.br. This is not 'critical' — it's 'on fire'. Token rotation should have happened before this roadmap review.

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.verdict: 15 minutes to rotate, 1 hour to document
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Go to Cloudflare dashboard → rotate API token → update wherever it's used (n8n, CI, Traefik). Then document the DNS state. Total: ~1 hour. The ROI is infinite because the downside is catastrophic.

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES — this is underrated. Should be item #0, not #7.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: YES. Only needs Cloudflare dashboard access.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: EXTREME. Every day with a possibly exposed token is a day an attacker can hijack your domain.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: traefik-ingress-hardening — Traefik cert automation depends on valid CF token.

now_items_analysis.title: Local Kubernetes and CI/CD parity for the full platform
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: Not started. Zero code, zero config. 'Model the final platform in local Kubernetes' is aspirational architecture before the VPS deployment is even stable.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: You have ONE service on k3s. 'Full platform parity' implies modeling every future service locally before deploying. This is waterfall thinking dressed as DevOps. Ship to VPS, iterate. Local parity is a luxury for teams with 10+ services and breaking deploys. You have neither.

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: Local K8s parity adds zero security value. The security surface is the VPS, not your laptop. Time spent here is time NOT spent on TLS, token rotation, or RLS policies.

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Not started, no deliverable defined, conflicts with item #1 (k8s-staged-environments). Solo developer doesn't need local K8s parity. Docker Compose for local dev, k3s for VPS. Done.
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.effort_vs_impact: Very high effort / Zero immediate impact

critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: NO. 'Local Docker parity stack validated end to end' = FALSE.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: ZERO. Nothing depends on this being 'now'.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: k8s-staged-environments (item #1) — overlapping scope, competing for infra attention.

now_items_analysis.title: OpenClaw — Claude Code first-class citizen in Pi ecosystem
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: AGENTS.md exists but /life/* endpoints don't. Pi local is NOT running (connection refused). The integration target is offline. Partial work exists but the runtime dependency is unavailable.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: You can't integrate with a service that's down. Pi local refuses connections on 127.0.0.1:3131. AGENTS.md is documentation, not integration. The dependency chain is: pi-local-app running → endpoints exist → Claude can call them. Step 1 is false. 7 downstream items depend on this (pi-life-manager-endpoints, pi-mobile-voice-refactory, pi-saas-commercialization, pi-mcp-server-public, etc). This is a critical-path bottleneck that's blocked.
anti_patterns_detected: 7-item dependency chain on an unavailable runtime

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.verdict: BLOCKED — but when unblocked, auth is priority
SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: When Pi local comes up: PI_API_KEY auth, session token validation, and the /session/sync PII surface all need review. But today? The service is down. Fix that first.

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Either: (A) fix pi-local-app to run → then this is valid, or (B) make downstream items not depend on it. Currently 7 items are blocked by a service that won't start. That's a 14% roadmap blockage from one dead process.
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.effort_vs_impact: Unknown effort (depends on why Pi is down) / Very high unblocking impact

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES in importance, NO in executability. Blocked by Pi local being offline.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: VERY HIGH. 7 downstream items are transitively blocked.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: No direct conflict, but its failure radiates to 14% of the roadmap.

now_items_analysis.title: Income Track A — Full-time remote job
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: INCOME_PIPELINE_2026-03-18.md exists with pitch letters. This is human-driven (jader-driven), not agent-dependent. The evidence is the pipeline document and platform registrations.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.verdict: OUT OF SCOPE for technical roadmap
RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: This is a career action item, not a technical deliverable. It doesn't belong in the same backlog as K8s namespaces and Traefik middleware. Having it here inflates the 'now' count and makes the technical roadmap look busier than it is. Track separately.
anti_patterns_detected: Mixing life goals with technical backlog

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: No security surface. Only risk: PII in pitch letters stored in repo. Ensure CV/cover letters with personal info aren't committed to public repos.
risks: PII in pitch documents if repo goes public

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.verdict: KEEP — this is the ACTUAL #1 priority
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Cashflow is the meta-blocker. Without income, the entire JPGLabs project is at risk. This should be item #1 in Jader's personal task list. But it shouldn't compete with technical items for 'now' slots in a technical roadmap.

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES for Jader. NO for the technical roadmap. Should be tracked separately.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: YES. Pipeline doc exists. Platforms accessible.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: Competes for Jader's TIME with all other items. Every hour on K8s is an hour not applying for jobs.

now_items_analysis.title: Income Track B — Freelance alto valor
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: Same as income-remote-fulltime. Human-driven, pipeline documented.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.verdict: SAME AS INCOME TRACK A — separate backlog
RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: Duplicate concern. This and Track A should be a single 'income' lane tracked outside the technical roadmap. Having two income items in 'now' makes the technical backlog look like it has 11 critical items when it really has 9 technical + 2 career.

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: Same PII concern as Track A.

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.verdict: KEEP but merge with Track A as single income lane
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: Both are 'apply for work daily'. The distinction between full-time and freelance is a Jader scheduling decision, not a roadmap item. One lane: 'income pipeline'. Daily cadence. Done.

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES for Jader. Merge with Track A.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: Lists income-remote-fulltime as dependency — odd. Freelance doesn't require having a full-time job first.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: HIGH. Same existential risk as Track A.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: income-remote-fulltime — false dependency. These are parallel activities, not sequential.

now_items_analysis.title: Homepage pública — seção de projetos, skills e CTA para contato
now_items_analysis.classification_rationale: Supabase seed data exists (7 projects, 25 skills). But the frontend page doesn't exist yet. Dependencies (portfolio-data-seeding, portfolio-seo-content) are both 'next' status, not 'now'.

RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT.reasoning: This is the correct end-goal: a public homepage that converts visitors to clients/employers. But its declared dependencies are both 'next' status items. Either promote those dependencies to 'now' or accept this item can't actually start. Also: priority_index 47 out of 50 contradicts 'critical now' status.

SECURITY_RELIABILITY.verdict: LOW RISK — it's a public page
SECURITY_RELIABILITY.reasoning: Public portfolio page has minimal security surface. Just ensure: no PII beyond professional info, no admin endpoints exposed, Supabase anon key RLS policies enforced.
risks: RLS policies must be verified before public launch

PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.reasoning: The income tracks need a portfolio to point to. Every job application says 'see my work at jpglabs.com.br'. If that URL shows nothing, applications are weaker. This is the single highest-ROI technical item for the income goal. Skip the fancy infra — hardcode data if needed, ship a page TODAY.
PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER.effort_vs_impact: Medium effort / Highest business impact (directly feeds income tracks)

critical_questions.really_critical_now: YES — arguably the most important technical item. But dependencies are unsatisfied.
critical_questions.dependencies_satisfied: NO. portfolio-data-seeding=next, portfolio-seo-content=next. However, seed data IS in Supabase already.
critical_questions.risk_of_stalling: HIGH. Income applications lack a portfolio to reference.
critical_questions.conflicts_with: dual-frontend-consolidation — which frontend hosts this page?

VERDICT.summary: The roadmap has 11 'now' items but only 4-5 are genuinely executable today. 2 are career items misplaced in a technical backlog. 2 are blocked by VPS SSH access. 1 is blocked by pi-local-app being offline. 1 should be demoted (local-k8s-ci-cd-parity). The priority_index ordering contradicts actual impact: portfolio-homepage-public is index 47 but should be the #1 technical deliverable given the income dependency.

scores.alignment_with_goals: 4/10 — income is the stated #1 goal but the portfolio that enables it is priority_index 47
scores.executability: 4/10 — SSH down, Pi local down, dependencies unmet on 6 of 11 items
scores.bloat_factor: HIGH — 25 items marked is_active_priority, 11 marked 'now', realistic 'now' capacity for solo dev is 3-4

hard_blockers.description: SSH timeout on port 22 blocks ALL VPS infrastructure work: traefik-ingress-hardening, k8s-staged-environments, n8n-ingress-deploy, cloudflare-dns-mail token rotation on services
hard_blockers.resolution: Contact Hostinger support OR fix via Hostinger web terminal (check iptables, sshd status, fail2ban)

hard_blockers.description: pi-local-app refuses connections on 127.0.0.1:3131. Blocks openclaw-pi-integration and its 7 transitive dependents.
hard_blockers.resolution: Debug pi-local-app startup. Check logs, port conflicts, Node/Bun process.

hard_blockers.description: Cloudflare API token possibly exposed. This is an active security incident, not a roadmap item.
items_blocked: All DNS-dependent items trust on domain integrity
hard_blockers.resolution: IMMEDIATE: Rotate token in Cloudflare dashboard. Update all consumers.

soft_blockers.description: Vite vs Next.js decision not documented. All frontend items proceed on implicit assumptions.
11 'now' items for 1 person. Realistic throughput: 2-3 items per week. Current burn rate: 0 items completed in 'now'.
items_blocked: Everything competes for the same human
soft_blockers.resolution: Ruthless prioritization. Max 3 'now' items at any time.

HIGH_RISK.description: Items where stalling >7 days creates exponential technical debt

items.explanation: Possibly exposed CF token. Every day is a day someone can hijack jpglabs.com.br DNS. Exponential because: if exploited, recovery requires re-establishing domain trust, re-issuing all TLS certs, notifying any users, potential Google/email blacklisting.

No TLS automation means manual cert management. Certs expire silently. No rate limiting means any script kiddie can overwhelm the VPS. Debt compounds: more services deployed without hardening = more exposure.
items.days_until_critical: Unknown — depends on cert expiry dates
items.action: Fix SSH → harden Traefik → automate TLS

items.explanation: 7 items transitively blocked. Every day this stays blocked, the dependency chain grows as new items reference Pi integration. The risk isn't just this item — it's the 14% of the roadmap that can't move.
items.action: Debug pi-local-app OR decouple downstream items

items.explanation: Income tracks A and B are active daily. Every application sent without a live portfolio is a weaker application. The opportunity cost compounds daily: N applications × reduced conversion rate × days without portfolio.
items.action: Ship MVP homepage even with hardcoded data

DEBATE.description: Points where the 3 personas fundamentally disagree

disagreements.topic: k8s-staged-environments: Build infra or ship product?
disagreements.RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT: Demote. 4-tier K8s is premature abstraction for 1 service.
disagreements.SECURITY_RELIABILITY: Keep — but narrow to STG only. Direct-to-PRD is a real risk.
disagreements.resolution: COMPROMISE: Create 1 STG namespace when SSH is restored. Defer DSV/TST indefinitely.

disagreements.topic: openclaw-pi-integration: Fix the runtime or decouple?
disagreements.RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT: Fix it. Pi is the architectural center. Decoupling creates orphaned services.
disagreements.SECURITY_RELIABILITY: Decouple. An offline dependency shouldn't block the entire roadmap.
disagreements.PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER: Decouple for now, fix Pi in background. Ship what you can without it.
disagreements.resolution: NO CONSENSUS. Depends on how fast Pi can be fixed. If <2 hours: fix. If unknown: decouple.

disagreements.topic: Income items in technical roadmap: Belong here or not?
disagreements.RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT: Remove. Career items pollute technical backlog semantics.
disagreements.SECURITY_RELIABILITY: Neutral. They don't affect security posture either way.
disagreements.PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER: Keep — they're the REASON the technical roadmap exists. Visibility matters.
disagreements.resolution: COMPROMISE: Move to separate 'Career' board/view. Keep cross-referenced via portfolio-homepage-public.

disagreements.topic: portfolio-homepage-public: Hardcode and ship or wait for Supabase wiring?
disagreements.RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT: Wait. Hardcoded data is tech debt. Wire Supabase first.
disagreements.SECURITY_RELIABILITY: Doesn't matter. Both approaches have same security surface.
disagreements.PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER: HARDCODE AND SHIP TODAY. A live page with static data beats a perfect page next month. Iterate.
disagreements.resolution: PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER wins. Ship with seed data or hardcoded. Refactor to live queries later.

disagreements.RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT: Some future value for reproducible deploys. But not now.
disagreements.PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER: Negative value — it consumes time that should go to shipping.

MINIMAL_FIX_PLAN.description: Top 5 actions ordered by impact/effort to unblock the roadmap in 48h

actions.how: Cloudflare dashboard → API Tokens → Rotate → Update in: n8n secrets, CI env vars, Traefik config. Can be done via browser, no SSH needed.

actions.impact: Unblocks all frontend items by making implicit architecture explicit
actions.how: Create docs/adr/001-dual-frontend-split.md. Decision: Vite=public portfolio, Next.js=authenticated dashboard. Done.

actions.action: Ship portfolio homepage MVP with seed data
actions.impact: Every job application now has a live portfolio URL. Directly boosts income tracks.
actions.how: Use existing Supabase seed data (7 projects, 25 skills). Build hero + projects grid + skills grid + CTA. Ship to Vite public frontend. Even hardcoded is fine for v1.
actions.persona_consensus: PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER + SECURITY_RELIABILITY agree. RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT accepts with 'refactor to live queries in sprint 2' caveat.

actions.how: Hostinger web terminal → check: systemctl status sshd, iptables -L, fail2ban-client status. Likely: fail2ban banned your IP or iptables rule dropped. Fix and test.
cd ~/code/pessoal/pi-local-app && check logs, port conflicts, .env. Start with: npm start or bun run. If persistent: check if port 3131 is held by zombie process.
actions.persona_consensus: RUTHLESS_ARCHITECT + PRAGMATIC_MAINTAINER agree. SECURITY_RELIABILITY says 'audit auth before reconnecting clients'.

MINIMAL_FIX_PLAN.48h_realistic_outcome: If all 5 actions complete: CF token rotated (security P0 resolved), ADR written (architecture unblocked), portfolio homepage live (income tracks strengthened), SSH restored (infra unblocked), Pi local running (integration chain unblocked). Roadmap goes from 4/10 executability to 7/10.

items_to_demote_immediately.reason: Not started, zero dependencies, solo dev doesn't need local K8s parity
Narrow to STG-only when SSH is restored. Full 4-tier is premature.
